D2 Vision L4 Analyze Reference

Anomaly & Daltonization

slide the deficiency severity, then toggle Daltonization to push lost contrast back into view

In anomalous trichromacy a cone's sensitivity is shifted, not absent, so red-green contrast is weakened by degrees. Daltonization recolours an image to route that lost contrast into a visible direction.

The top row is the true palette; the bottom is a deuteranomalous observer's view. Slide the severity to fade the red-green pairs together, then toggle Daltonization and watch the bottom row regain its distinctions.
